concepts.description: How unhandled errors propagate through async call chains and why missing try/catch in async functions is a production-critical bug

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify what happens when an async function throws without try/catch
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the missing error handling in a real async chain
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a production error handling strategy for async pipelines

concepts.description: Composing multiple async operations sequentially using .then() chains, async/await sequences, or combinators

difficulty_notes.junior: Trace the execution order of chained async calls
difficulty_notes.mid: Spot a race condition or dropped error in a chain
difficulty_notes.senior: Refactor a .then() chain for readability and error resilience

concepts.description: Running multiple async operations in parallel with Promise.all, tokio::join!, asyncio.gather, or WaitGroup and handling partial failures

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify which API runs tasks concurrently vs sequentially
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the bug when one of N concurrent tasks fails
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a fault-tolerant fan-out/fan-in pipeline

concepts.description: Passing data between concurrent units via channels (Go chan, Rust mpsc, Python asyncio.Queue) instead of shared mutable state

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify whether a channel is buffered or unbuffered
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace data flow through a producer-consumer channel pipeline
difficulty_notes.senior: Compare channel-based vs shared-memory approaches for a real workload

concepts.description: Propagating cancellation signals through async call trees using context.Context, AbortController, CancellationToken, or select with timeout

difficulty_notes.junior: Name the mechanism used to cancel an async operation in the language
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the missing cancellation propagation in nested async calls
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a timeout strategy for a multi-service request pipeline

concepts.description: Comparing exception-based error handling (try/catch/throw) with value-based error handling (Result<T,E>, Option<T>, Either) and when each is appropriate

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify whether the code uses exception-based or value-based errors
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the error case that silently falls through
difficulty_notes.senior: Argue for exception vs result-type in a given architecture

concepts.description: Language-specific idioms for forwarding errors up the call stack: ? operator (Rust), if err != nil (Go), throws (Java/Swift), raise/throw (Python/JS)

difficulty_notes.junior: Fill in the correct error propagation syntax for the language
difficulty_notes.mid: Find where an error is swallowed instead of propagated
difficulty_notes.senior: Design error boundaries for a layered architecture

concepts.description: Defining application-specific error types (enum AppError, class HttpError extends Error) to distinguish operational vs programmer errors

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify which error type matches a given failure scenario
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace which catch block handles a specific error
difficulty_notes.senior: Design an error taxonomy for a multi-layer application

concepts.description: Continuing with reduced functionality when a dependency fails: circuit breakers, fallback values, retry with backoff, partial response patterns

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify whether a failure mode is graceful or catastrophic
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the missing fallback in a service dependency chain
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a resilience strategy with circuit breaker and retry policies

concepts.description: Restricting a class to a single instance with global access point — understanding the trade-offs with testability and hidden dependencies

difficulty_notes.junior: Name this creational pattern from a getInstance() code sample
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the thread-safety or state-leak bug in a singleton implementation
difficulty_notes.senior: Critique singleton usage and propose dependency injection alternative

concepts.description: Decoupling publishers from subscribers using on/emit, addEventListener, or pub/sub to react to state changes without tight coupling

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify the observer pattern from on/emit usage
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the memory leak from missing unsubscribe
difficulty_notes.senior: Compare observer vs reactive streams for event-heavy systems

concepts.description: Abstracting object creation so the calling code doesn't depend on concrete classes — enables swapping implementations without changing consumers

difficulty_notes.junior: Recognize a factory method from code and name the pattern
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace which concrete type a factory produces given specific input
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a factory that supports runtime registration of new types

concepts.description: Encapsulating interchangeable algorithms behind a common interface so the algorithm can be swapped at runtime without modifying the client

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify strategy pattern from interchangeable function/interface usage
difficulty_notes.mid: Fill in the interface/function signature that makes strategies swappable
difficulty_notes.senior: Refactor a switch-case into strategy pattern and discuss trade-offs

concepts.description: Constructing complex objects step-by-step with a fluent API instead of telescoping constructors or large parameter lists

difficulty_notes.junior: Trace a builder chain to determine the final object configuration
difficulty_notes.mid: Fill in the missing method that makes the builder chain compile
difficulty_notes.senior: Compare builder vs constructor overloads vs named parameters

concepts.description: Processing requests through a pipeline of handlers where each handler decides whether to pass, modify, or short-circuit the request

difficulty_notes.junior: Arrange middleware handlers in the correct execution order
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the middleware that fails to call next() and blocks the pipeline
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a middleware architecture with error handling and logging

concepts.description: Providing dependencies to a component from outside rather than having it create them internally — enables testing, loose coupling, and swappable implementations

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify constructor injection from a class that accepts interfaces
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the hard-coded dependency that breaks testability
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a DI container configuration for a multi-module application

concepts.description: Separating concerns into Controller → Service → Repository layers where each layer only calls the one below it

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify which layer a given class belongs to based on its responsibilities
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the layer violation where a controller directly queries the database
difficulty_notes.senior: Design the layer boundaries and inter-layer contracts for a new feature

concepts.description: Framework-level request processing pipeline (Express middleware, ASP.NET middleware, Rack) where order matters and each step can short-circuit

difficulty_notes.junior: Arrange auth → logging → rate-limit → handler in correct order
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the middleware ordering bug that causes auth bypass
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a middleware pipeline with proper error propagation and metrics

concepts.description: Decoupling services through asynchronous events instead of synchronous API calls — event sourcing, message queues, eventual consistency

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify whether a system uses event-driven or request-response communication
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace the event flow and determine the final state after N events
difficulty_notes.senior: Design an event sourcing system with replay and compensating events

concepts.description: Patterns for inter-service communication: REST, gRPC, message queues, service mesh — and the distributed systems problems each introduces

difficulty_notes.junior: Name the communication pattern (sync REST vs async message queue)
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the missing retry/timeout that causes cascading failure
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a communication strategy for a specific microservice topology

concepts.description: Keeping interfaces small and focused so implementors aren't forced to depend on methods they don't use — the I in SOLID

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify whether an interface follows or violates ISP
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the fat interface that forces empty method implementations
difficulty_notes.senior: Refactor a bloated interface into role-specific interfaces

concepts.description: Preferring delegation and contained objects over class hierarchies to avoid the fragile base class problem and enable flexible behavior combinations

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify whether code uses inheritance or composition
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace method resolution in a multi-level inheritance chain
difficulty_notes.senior: Refactor a deep inheritance hierarchy into a composition-based design

concepts.description: Implementing behavior contracts (Rust traits, Go interfaces, C# interfaces, Python protocols) for polymorphism without inheritance

difficulty_notes.junior: Fill in the trait/interface method signature to satisfy the contract
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the missing trait implementation that causes a compile error
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a trait hierarchy for a plugin system

concepts.description: Sharing behavior across unrelated classes using include/extend (Ruby), mixins (Python), or traits (PHP/Scala) without classical inheritance

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify that include/extend adds methods to a class
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace method resolution when multiple modules define the same method
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a mixin architecture that avoids method name collisions

concepts.description: Dynamic dispatch (virtual methods, trait objects) vs static dispatch (generics, monomorphization) and their performance/flexibility trade-offs

difficulty_notes.junior: Predict which method implementation runs given a polymorphic call
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the incorrect downcast or missing virtual keyword
difficulty_notes.senior: Compare dyn Trait vs generic <T: Trait> for a real API

concepts.description: Functions that take functions as arguments or return functions — map/filter/reduce, decorators, middleware factories

difficulty_notes.junior: Trace map/filter/reduce to determine the output
difficulty_notes.mid: Fill in the predicate function that produces the expected result
difficulty_notes.senior: Compose multiple HOFs into a data processing pipeline and discuss readability

concepts.description: Functions that capture variables from their enclosing scope — understanding what is captured (value vs reference) and lifetime implications

difficulty_notes.junior: Trace a closure's output given captured variable values
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the stale closure bug in a loop or React effect
difficulty_notes.senior: Analyze ownership/move semantics in Rust closures vs JS closures

concepts.description: Writing functions without side effects and using immutable data structures to make code predictable, testable, and safe for concurrency

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify whether a function is pure or has side effects
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the hidden mutation that causes a bug in a supposedly pure function
difficulty_notes.senior: Refactor a stateful module into pure functions + immutable state

concepts.description: Destructuring and branching on data shape (Rust match, Python match/case, C# switch expressions) with exhaustiveness checking

difficulty_notes.junior: Trace which match arm executes for a given input
difficulty_notes.mid: Fill in the missing pattern that handles an unmatched case
difficulty_notes.senior: Design exhaustive pattern matching for a domain-specific enum

concepts.description: Launching lightweight concurrent units of execution (goroutines, tokio tasks, green threads) and understanding their lifecycle and resource implications

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify that goroutines/tasks are not OS threads
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the goroutine leak from a missing done channel or context
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a bounded concurrency system with backpressure

concepts.description: Protecting shared mutable state with mutexes, read-write locks, or synchronized blocks — and understanding the performance vs safety trade-off

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify why concurrent access to a shared variable needs a lock
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the race condition from a missing or incorrectly scoped lock
difficulty_notes.senior: Replace lock-based synchronization with lock-free data structures

concepts.description: Isolating state within actors that communicate only via message passing — Erlang/Elixir processes, Akka actors, or actor-like patterns in Go/Rust

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify the actor model from message-passing code
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace message flow between actors to determine final state
difficulty_notes.senior: Design an actor-based system with supervision and restart policies

concepts.description: Bounding parallelism with a fixed pool of workers consuming from a shared job queue — preventing resource exhaustion under load

difficulty_notes.junior: Arrange the steps to set up a worker pool correctly
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the missing synchronization that causes workers to exit early
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a dynamic worker pool with autoscaling and graceful shutdown

concepts.description: Recognizing and preventing circular wait conditions: lock ordering, timeout-based acquisition, try-lock patterns, and deadlock detection strategies

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify the four conditions required for deadlock
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the lock ordering violation that causes a deadlock
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a lock-free alternative or prove deadlock freedom

concepts.description: Avoiding the N+1 problem, choosing eager vs lazy loading, understanding when to drop to raw SQL, and reading generated queries

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify what the N+1 problem is from a code sample
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the N+1 query hidden in a loop over ORM relations
difficulty_notes.senior: Refactor a view with 5 queries into 1 using eager loading + projection

concepts.description: Schema evolution with reversible migrations, zero-downtime deployment, and handling data transformations alongside schema changes

difficulty_notes.junior: Arrange migration steps in correct order (add column → backfill → remove old)
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the migration that breaks existing reads during deployment
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a zero-downtime migration for a column rename with active traffic

concepts.description: Sizing connection pools, handling idle timeout, connection leaks, and understanding how pool exhaustion causes cascading failures

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify why a connection pool is used instead of opening new connections
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the connection leak from missing close/defer/using
difficulty_notes.senior: Size a connection pool for a given traffic pattern and analyze failure modes

concepts.description: Defining where transactions start and end, understanding isolation levels, and ensuring all-or-nothing semantics for multi-step operations

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify what ACID means in a transaction context
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the missing rollback that leaves data in an inconsistent state
difficulty_notes.senior: Design transaction boundaries for a saga across multiple services

concepts.description: Abstracting data access behind an interface so business logic doesn't depend on the specific database technology or query language

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify the repository pattern from a class with CRUD methods
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace which repository implementation is used via dependency injection
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a generic repository with specification pattern for complex queries

concepts.description: Designing URL hierarchies, choosing HTTP methods, returning correct status codes, and handling pagination/filtering for REST APIs

difficulty_notes.junior: Match HTTP methods to CRUD operations
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the REST convention violation (wrong status code, wrong method)
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a resource hierarchy for a complex domain with nested resources

concepts.description: Implementing authentication/authorization as middleware that validates tokens, extracts user context, and enforces role-based access

difficulty_notes.junior: Arrange auth middleware steps: verify token → extract user → check role → proceed
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the auth bypass from incorrect middleware ordering or missing check
difficulty_notes.senior: Design an auth system supporting multiple providers and fine-grained permissions

concepts.description: Protecting APIs from abuse using token bucket, sliding window, or leaky bucket algorithms — and returning appropriate 429 responses with Retry-After

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify which rate limiting algorithm is being used
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace a token bucket to determine if a request is allowed or throttled
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a distributed rate limiter for a multi-instance API

concepts.description: Managing breaking changes in APIs using URL prefix (/v1/), header-based, or content negotiation versioning — and deprecation policies

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify the versioning strategy from URL patterns or headers
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the breaking change that wasn't versioned
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a versioning and deprecation strategy for a public API

concepts.description: Testing units in isolation using mocks, stubs, and fakes to replace real dependencies — and understanding when mocking harms more than helps

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify why a test uses a mock instead of the real database
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the test that passes with mocks but fails in integration
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a testing strategy balancing mocks vs real dependencies

concepts.description: Testing real interactions between components with actual databases, APIs, and message queues — using test containers, fixtures, and seed data

difficulty_notes.junior: Distinguish integration tests from unit tests by their characteristics
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the test pollution where one test's data affects another
difficulty_notes.senior: Design an integration test suite with proper isolation and CI performance

concepts.description: Writing tests before implementation code in the red-green-refactor cycle — and understanding when TDD adds value vs overhead

difficulty_notes.junior: Arrange TDD steps in correct order: write failing test → implement → refactor
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the test that was written after the code (tests implementation, not behavior)
difficulty_notes.senior: Argue for or against TDD for a greenfield vs legacy codebase

concepts.description: Testing invariants across randomly generated inputs instead of hand-picked examples — using Hypothesis, fast-check, or proptest

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify what makes property-based testing different from example-based testing
difficulty_notes.mid: Write the property invariant for a given function
difficulty_notes.senior: Design property tests for a stateful system with model-based testing

concepts.description: Setting up and tearing down test state using fixtures, factories, builders, and shared contexts — avoiding test coupling and pollution

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify the purpose of setUp/tearDown or before/after hooks
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the shared mutable fixture that makes test order matter
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a fixture strategy for a test suite with 500+ tests

concepts.description: Validating and sanitizing all external input (user forms, API payloads, query params) to prevent injection attacks, XSS, and data corruption

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify why raw user input should never be used in a SQL query
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the XSS or injection vulnerability in a request handler
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a validation layer using schema validation for a multi-endpoint API

concepts.description: JWT lifecycle (issue → validate → refresh → revoke), secure token storage, and understanding the trade-offs between JWTs and session cookies

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify the components of a JWT (header.payload.signature)
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the JWT validation bug that allows expired or tampered tokens
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a token refresh strategy with revocation for a mobile app

concepts.description: Understanding same-origin policy, configuring CORS headers correctly, and implementing CSRF tokens to prevent cross-site attacks

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify what CORS prevents and when it applies
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the overly permissive CORS configuration (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *)
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a CORS + CSRF strategy for a multi-domain SPA architecture

concepts.description: Keeping API keys, database credentials, and encryption keys out of source code using environment variables, vaults, and rotation policies

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify why hardcoded secrets in source code are dangerous
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the secret that leaked through logging, error messages, or git history
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a secrets management strategy with rotation and least-privilege access

concepts.description: Centralizing application state in a single store (Zustand, Redux, Vuex) with predictable mutations and derived state via selectors

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify the state management library from store setup code
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the unnecessary re-render caused by non-memoized selector
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a state architecture for a complex multi-feature application

concepts.description: Subscribing to state changes and reacting automatically — observables, signals, computed properties, and subscription lifecycle management

difficulty_notes.junior: Fill in the subscribe/unsubscribe calls for a state change listener
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the subscription memory leak from missing cleanup
difficulty_notes.senior: Compare signals vs observables vs hooks for a real-time dashboard

concepts.description: Distinguishing server-owned data (cached, stale, refetchable) from client-owned state (UI, form, local preferences) and using appropriate tools for each

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify whether a piece of data is server state or client state
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the bug from treating server data as client state (stale cache, no refetch)
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a data fetching strategy with optimistic updates and cache invalidation

concepts.description: Writing type-safe reusable code with generics/templates — type constraints, variance, and understanding when generics add value vs complexity

difficulty_notes.junior: Trace a generic function's type parameter for a specific invocation
difficulty_notes.mid: Fill in the generic constraint that makes the function compile
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a generic repository/collection with proper variance annotations

concepts.description: Safely working with values that might not exist using Option<T>, nullable references, optional chaining (?.), and null coalescing (??)

difficulty_notes.junior: Trace optional chaining to determine if a value is null or defined
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the null reference exception from missing null check
difficulty_notes.senior: Compare Option/Result monads vs nullable types for API design

concepts.description: Refining a broad type to a specific one using typeof, instanceof, discriminated unions, or custom type predicates for safe type-specific operations

difficulty_notes.junior: Trace a type guard to determine the narrowed type in each branch
difficulty_notes.mid: Fill in the type predicate that correctly narrows a union type
difficulty_notes.senior: Design a discriminated union with exhaustive handling for a domain model

concepts.description: Managing side effects in React components: dependency arrays, cleanup functions, avoiding infinite loops, and handling async effects correctly

difficulty_notes.junior: Fill in the cleanup function that prevents a memory leak
difficulty_notes.mid: Find the missing dependency or infinite loop in a useEffect
difficulty_notes.senior: Critique useEffect vs custom hooks vs server components for data fetching

concepts.description: Building flexible UI components using composition patterns: children, render props, compound components, and the slot pattern — vs prop drilling

difficulty_notes.junior: Identify the composition pattern used in a component tree
difficulty_notes.mid: Trace data flow through render props or context to find the rendered output
difficulty_notes.senior: Refactor a prop-drilling component tree into a compound component API
